revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagara Cave Tomb cluster | native_name =長柄横穴群 | native_name_lang = | image = 長柄横穴群8号墓.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagara Cave Tomb No.8 | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagara Cave Tomb cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|58|N|140|14|24|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagara, Chiba]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nagara Cave Tomb cluster'''|長柄横穴群|Nagara yokoana-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a group of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs located in the Tokumasu neighborhood of the town of [[Nagara, Chiba|Nagara]] in [[Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These tombs have been protected as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1995.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162391|title=長柄横穴群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Corridor-type ''kofun'' appeared in the [[Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō region]] from the latter half of the 6th century AD and about 4000 examples have been found in Chiba Prefecture. The group at Nagara was made in the mid-7th and early 8th century AD, and consists of 324 tombs in 25 groups, distributed in the southern part of Nagara town. The style in this region is unique and is called the "Kodanshiki," in which the burial chamber is a smaller room located at the rear of an entrance room and elevated slightly above it. The largest tomb has difference in height between these rooms of 2.9 meters. The entrance rooms can roughly be divided into what is called an "arch type", in which the cross section of the ceiling shows a semi-cylindrical shape, and what is called a "house type" in which the ceiling and walls mimic the roof and walls of a house. In addition, there are a few which are "dome type" with a domed ceiling. Some of the tombs have a casket platform, on which the wooden sarcophagus once rested.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the tombs, designated No.13, has a line engraving on its back wall depicting people, birds, houses, boats, and what appears to be a five-story [[Japanese pagoda]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tombs have been opened since antiquity, and few [[grave goods]] have been recovered, other than fragments of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and fragments of iron tools or weapons.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a 15-minute walk from the Tokumasu bus stop on the Kominato Bus from [[Mobara Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Sotobo Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> 長柄横穴群2号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群2号墓玄室.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.11 長柄横穴群16号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.16 長柄横穴群19号墓玄室.JPG| NagaraTomb No.19 </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Nagara Yokoana Group}} *[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n411-032.html Chiba Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.town.nagara.chiba.jp/uploaded/attachment/366.pdf Nagara Town Home Page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagara Cave Tomb cluster | native_name =長柄横穴群 | native_name_lang = | image = 長柄横穴群8号墓.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagara Cave Tomb No.8 | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagara Cave Tomb cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|58|N|140|14|24|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagara, Chiba]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nagara Cave Tomb cluster'''|長柄横穴群|Nagara yokoana-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a group of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs located in the Tokumasu neighborhood of the town of [[Nagara, Chiba|Nagara]] in [[Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. These tombs have been protected as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1995.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162391|title=長柄横穴群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Corridor-type ''kofun'' appeared in the [[Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō region]] from the latter half of the 6th century AD and about 4000 examples have been found in Chiba Prefecture. The group at Nagara was made in the mid-7th and early 8th century AD, and consists of 324 tombs in 25 groups, distributed in the southern part of Nagara town. The style in this region is unique and is called the "Kodanshiki," in which the burial chamber is a smaller room located at the rear of an entrance room and elevated slightly above it. The largest tomb has difference in height between these rooms of 2.9 meters. The entrance rooms can roughly be divided into what is called an "arch type", in which the cross section of the ceiling shows a semi-cylindrical shape, and what is called a "house type" in which the ceiling and walls mimic the roof and walls of a house. In addition, there are a few which are "dome type" with a domed ceiling. Some of the tombs have a casket platform, on which the wooden sarcophagus once rested.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the tombs, designated No.13, has a line engraving on its back wall depicting people, birds, houses, boats, and what appears to be a five-story [[Japanese pagoda]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tombs have been opened since antiquity, and few [[grave goods]] have been recovered, other than fragments of [[Sue pottery]] and [[Haji pottery]] pottery and fragments of iron tools or weapons.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a 15-minute walk from the Tokumasu bus stop on the Kominato Bus from [[Mobara Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Sotobo Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> 長柄横穴群2号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群2号墓玄室.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.11 長柄横穴群16号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.16 長柄横穴群19号墓玄室.JPG| NagaraTomb No.19 </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Nagara Yokoana Group}} *[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/n411-032.html Chiba Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.town.nagara.chiba.jp/uploaded/attachment/366.pdf Nagara Town Home Page] {{in lang|ja}}

These tombs have been protected as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1995.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162391|title=長柄横穴群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

The largest tomb has difference in height between these rooms of 2.9 meters. The entrance rooms can roughly be divided into what is called an "arch type", in which the cross section of the ceiling shows a semi-cylindrical shape, and what is called a "house type" in which the ceiling and walls mimic the roof and walls of a house. In addition, there are a few which are "dome type" with a domed ceiling. Some of the tombs have a casket platform, on which the wooden sarcophagus once rested.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nagara Yokoana Group]][[en:Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:長柄横穴群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226522]] Wikidata name: Nagara Ruins [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagara Cave Tomb cluster | native_name =長柄横穴群 | native_name_lang = | image =長柄横穴群8号墓.JPG|image= File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagara Cave Tomb No.8 | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagara Cave Tomb cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|25|58|N|140|14|24|E||display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.432972|N|140.24025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nagara, Chiba|Nagara, Chiba]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nagara Cave Tomb cluster'''|長柄横穴群|Nagara yokoana-gun}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] containing a group of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs located in the Tokumasu neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Nagara, Chiba|Nagara]] in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. These tombs have been protected as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1995.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162391|title=長柄横穴群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Corridor-type ''kofun'' appeared in the [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō region]] from the latter half of the 6th century AD and about 4000 examples have been found in Chiba Prefecture. The group at Nagara was made in the mid-7th and early 8th century AD, and consists of 324 tombs in 25 groups, distributed in the southern part of Nagara town. The style in this region is unique and is called the "Kodanshiki," in which the burial chamber is a smaller room located at the rear of an entrance room and elevated slightly above it. The largest tomb has difference in height between these rooms of 2.9 meters. The entrance rooms can roughly be divided into what is called an "arch type", in which the cross section of the ceiling shows a semi-cylindrical shape, and what is called a "house type" in which the ceiling and walls mimic the roof and walls of a house. In addition, there are a few which are "dome type" with a domed ceiling. Some of the tombs have a casket platform, on which the wooden sarcophagus once rested.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the tombs, designated No.13, has a line engraving on its back wall depicting people, birds, houses, boats, and what appears to be a five-story [[Japanese pagoda]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tombs have been opened since antiquity, and few [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been recovered, other than fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery and fragments of iron tools or weapons.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a 15-minute walk from the Tokumasu bus stop on the Kominato Bus from [[:en:Mobara Station|Mobara Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Sotobo Line|Sotobo Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> 長柄横穴群2号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群2号墓玄室.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.11 長柄横穴群16号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.16 長柄横穴群19号墓玄室.JPG| NagaraTomb No.19 </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nagara Yokoana Group]][[en:Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:長柄横穴群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226522]] Wikidata name: Nagara Ruins [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagara Cave Tomb cluster | native_name =長柄横穴群 | native_name_lang = | image =長柄横穴群8号墓.JPG|image= File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagara Cave Tomb No.8 | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagara Cave Tomb cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|25|58|N|140|14|24|E||display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.432972|N|140.24025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nagara, Chiba|Nagara, Chiba]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nagara Cave Tomb cluster'''|長柄横穴群|Nagara yokoana-gun}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] containing a group of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs located in the Tokumasu neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Nagara, Chiba|Nagara]] in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. These tombs have been protected as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1995.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162391|title=長柄横穴群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Corridor-type ''kofun'' appeared in the [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō region]] from the latter half of the 6th century AD and about 4000 examples have been found in Chiba Prefecture. The group at Nagara was made in the mid-7th and early 8th century AD, and consists of 324 tombs in 25 groups, distributed in the southern part of Nagara town. The style in this region is unique and is called the "Kodanshiki," in which the burial chamber is a smaller room located at the rear of an entrance room and elevated slightly above it. The largest tomb has difference in height between these rooms of 2.9 meters. The entrance rooms can roughly be divided into what is called an "arch type", in which the cross section of the ceiling shows a semi-cylindrical shape, and what is called a "house type" in which the ceiling and walls mimic the roof and walls of a house. In addition, there are a few which are "dome type" with a domed ceiling. Some of the tombs have a casket platform, on which the wooden sarcophagus once rested.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the tombs, designated No.13, has a line engraving on its back wall depicting people, birds, houses, boats, and what appears to be a five-story [[Japanese pagoda]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tombs have been opened since antiquity, and few [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been recovered, other than fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery and fragments of iron tools or weapons.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a 15-minute walk from the Tokumasu bus stop on the Kominato Bus from [[:en:Mobara Station|Mobara Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Sotobo Line|Sotobo Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> 長柄横穴群2号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群2号墓玄室.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.11 長柄横穴群16号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.16 長柄横穴群19号墓玄室.JPG| NagaraTomb No.19 </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nagara Yokoana Group]][[en:Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:長柄横穴群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nagara Yokoana Group]][[en:Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:長柄横穴群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nagara Yokoana Group]][[en:Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:長柄横穴群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226522]] Wikidata name: Nagara Ruins [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagara Cave Tomb cluster | native_name =長柄横穴群 | native_name_lang = | image =長柄横穴群8号墓.JPG|image= File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagara Cave Tomb No.8 | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagara Cave Tomb cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|25|58|N|140|14|24|E||display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.432972|N|140.24025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nagara, Chiba|Nagara, Chiba]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nagara Cave Tomb cluster'''|長柄横穴群|Nagara yokoana-gun}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] containing a group of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs located in the Tokumasu neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Nagara, Chiba|Nagara]] in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. These tombs have been protected as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1995.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162391|title=長柄横穴群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Corridor-type ''kofun'' appeared in the [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō region]] from the latter half of the 6th century AD and about 4000 examples have been found in Chiba Prefecture. The group at Nagara was made in the mid-7th and early 8th century AD, and consists of 324 tombs in 25 groups, distributed in the southern part of Nagara town. The style in this region is unique and is called the "Kodanshiki," in which the burial chamber is a smaller room located at the rear of an entrance room and elevated slightly above it. The largest tomb has difference in height between these rooms of 2.9 meters. The entrance rooms can roughly be divided into what is called an "arch type", in which the cross section of the ceiling shows a semi-cylindrical shape, and what is called a "house type" in which the ceiling and walls mimic the roof and walls of a house. In addition, there are a few which are "dome type" with a domed ceiling. Some of the tombs have a casket platform, on which the wooden sarcophagus once rested.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the tombs, designated No.13, has a line engraving on its back wall depicting people, birds, houses, boats, and what appears to be a five-story [[Japanese pagoda]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tombs have been opened since antiquity, and few [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been recovered, other than fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery and fragments of iron tools or weapons.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a 15-minute walk from the Tokumasu bus stop on the Kominato Bus from [[:en:Mobara Station|Mobara Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Sotobo Line|Sotobo Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> 長柄横穴群2号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群2号墓玄室.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.11 長柄横穴群16号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.16 長柄横穴群19号墓玄室.JPG| NagaraTomb No.19 </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nagara Yokoana Group]][[en:Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:長柄横穴群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nagara Yokoana Group]][[en:Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:長柄横穴群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nagara Yokoana Group]][[en:Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:長柄横穴群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226522]] Wikidata name: Nagara Ruins [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagara Cave Tomb cluster | native_name =長柄横穴群 | native_name_lang = | image =長柄横穴群8号墓.JPG|image= File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagara Cave Tomb No.8 | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagara Cave Tomb cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|25|58|N|140|14|24|E||display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.432972|N|140.24025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nagara, Chiba|Nagara, Chiba]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nagara Cave Tomb cluster'''|長柄横穴群|Nagara yokoana-gun}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] containing a group of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs located in the Tokumasu neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Nagara, Chiba|Nagara]] in [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. These tombs have been protected as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1995.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162391|title=長柄横穴群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Corridor-type ''kofun'' appeared in the [[:en:Bōsō Peninsula|Bōsō region]] from the latter half of the 6th century AD and about 4000 examples have been found in Chiba Prefecture. The group at Nagara was made in the mid-7th and early 8th century AD, and consists of 324 tombs in 25 groups, distributed in the southern part of Nagara town. The style in this region is unique and is called the "Kodanshiki," in which the burial chamber is a smaller room located at the rear of an entrance room and elevated slightly above it. The largest tomb has difference in height between these rooms of 2.9 meters. The entrance rooms can roughly be divided into what is called an "arch type", in which the cross section of the ceiling shows a semi-cylindrical shape, and what is called a "house type" in which the ceiling and walls mimic the roof and walls of a house. In addition, there are a few which are "dome type" with a domed ceiling. Some of the tombs have a casket platform, on which the wooden sarcophagus once rested.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the tombs, designated No.13, has a line engraving on its back wall depicting people, birds, houses, boats, and what appears to be a five-story [[Japanese pagoda]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tombs have been opened since antiquity, and few [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been recovered, other than fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery and fragments of iron tools or weapons.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a 15-minute walk from the Tokumasu bus stop on the Kominato Bus from [[:en:Mobara Station|Mobara Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Sotobo Line|Sotobo Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> 長柄横穴群2号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群2号墓玄室.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.11 長柄横穴群16号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.16 長柄横穴群19号墓玄室.JPG| NagaraTomb No.19 </gallery>

The {{nihongo|'''Nagara Cave Tomb cluster'''|長柄横穴群|Nagara yokoana-gun}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site}} containing a group of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs located in the Tokumasu neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|Nagara, Chiba|WD=Q1203545|lt=Nagara}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. These tombs have been protected as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1995.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162391|title=長柄横穴群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Corridor-type ''kofun'' appeared in the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|WD=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō region}} from the latter half of the 6th century AD and about 4000 examples have been found in Chiba Prefecture. The group at Nagara was made in the mid-7th and early 8th century AD, and consists of 324 tombs in 25 groups, distributed in the southern part of Nagara town. The style in this region is unique and is called the "Kodanshiki," in which the burial chamber is a smaller room located at the rear of an entrance room and elevated slightly above it. The largest tomb has difference in height between these rooms of 2.9 meters. The entrance rooms can roughly be divided into what is called an "arch type", in which the cross section of the ceiling shows a semi-cylindrical shape, and what is called a "house type" in which the ceiling and walls mimic the roof and walls of a house. In addition, there are a few which are "dome type" with a domed ceiling. Some of the tombs have a casket platform, on which the wooden sarcophagus once rested.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The tombs have been opened since antiquity, and few {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been recovered, other than fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery and fragments of iron tools or weapons.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Some of the tombs have a casket platform, on which the wooden sarcophagus once rested.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the tombs, designated No.13, has a line engraving on its back wall depicting people, birds, houses, boats, and what appears to be a five-story [[Japanese pagoda]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tombs have been opened since antiquity, and few {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been recovered, other than fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery and fragments of iron tools or weapons.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a 15-minute walk from the Tokumasu bus stop on the Kominato Bus from {{ill|Mobara Station|en|Mobara Station|WD=Q564427|lt=Mobara Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Sotobo Line|Sotobo Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> 長柄横穴群2号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群2号墓玄室.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.11 長柄横穴群16号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.16 長柄横穴群19号墓玄室.JPG| NagaraTomb No.19 </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagara Cave Tomb cluster | native_name =長柄横穴群 | native_name_lang = | image =長柄横穴群8号墓.JPG|image= File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagara Cave Tomb No.8 | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagara Cave Tomb cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|25|58|N|140|14|24|E||display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.432972|N|140.24025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|Nagara, Chiba|WD=Q1203545|lt=Nagara, Chiba}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} | type = tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nagara Cave Tomb cluster'''|長柄横穴群|Nagara yokoana-gun}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site}} containing a group of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave Tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs located in the Tokumasu neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|Nagara, Chiba|WD=Q1203545|lt=Nagara}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. These tombs have been protected as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1995.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162391|title=長柄横穴群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Corridor-type ''kofun'' appeared in the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|WD=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō region}} from the latter half of the 6th century AD and about 4000 examples have been found in Chiba Prefecture. The group at Nagara was made in the mid-7th and early 8th century AD, and consists of 324 tombs in 25 groups, distributed in the southern part of Nagara town. The style in this region is unique and is called the "Kodanshiki," in which the burial chamber is a smaller room located at the rear of an entrance room and elevated slightly above it. The largest tomb has difference in height between these rooms of 2.9 meters. The entrance rooms can roughly be divided into what is called an "arch type", in which the cross section of the ceiling shows a semi-cylindrical shape, and what is called a "house type" in which the ceiling and walls mimic the roof and walls of a house. In addition, there are a few which are "dome type" with a domed ceiling. Some of the tombs have a casket platform, on which the wooden sarcophagus once rested.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the tombs, designated No.13, has a line engraving on its back wall depicting people, birds, houses, boats, and what appears to be a five-story [[Japanese pagoda]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tombs have been opened since antiquity, and few {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been recovered, other than fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery and fragments of iron tools or weapons.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a 15-minute walk from the Tokumasu bus stop on the Kominato Bus from {{ill|Mobara Station|en|Mobara Station|WD=Q564427|lt=Mobara Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Sotobo Line|Sotobo Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Nagara Yokoanabo cluster'''|長柄横穴群|Nagara yokoana-gun}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} containing a group of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs located in the Tokumasu neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|Nagara, Chiba|WD=Q1203545|lt=Nagara|1=Nagara, Chiba}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. These tombs have been protected as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1995.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162391|title=長柄横穴群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Corridor-type ''kofun'' appeared in the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|WD=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō region|1=Bōsō Peninsula}} from the latter half of the 6th century AD and about 4000 examples have been found in Chiba Prefecture. The group at Nagara was made in the mid-7th and early 8th century AD, and consists of 324 tombs in 25 groups, distributed in the southern part of Nagara town. The style in this region is unique and is called the "Kodanshiki," in which the burial chamber is a smaller room located at the rear of an entrance room and elevated slightly above it. The largest tomb has difference in height between these rooms of 2.9 meters. The entrance rooms can roughly be divided into what is called an "arch type", in which the cross section of the ceiling shows a semi-cylindrical shape, and what is called a "house type" in which the ceiling and walls mimic the roof and walls of a house. In addition, there are a few which are "dome type" with a domed ceiling. Some of the tombs have a casket platform, on which the wooden sarcophagus once rested.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the tombs, designated No.13, has a line engraving on its back wall depicting people, birds, houses, boats, and what appears to be a five-story [[Japanese pagoda]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tombs have been opened since antiquity, and few {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been recovered, other than fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery and fragments of iron tools or weapons.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a 15-minute walk from the Tokumasu bus stop on the Kominato Bus from {{ill|Mobara Station|en|Mobara Station|WD=Q564427|lt=Mobara Station|1=Mobara Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Sotobo Line|Sotobo Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> 長柄横穴群2号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群2号墓玄室.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.2 長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.11 長柄横穴群16号墓.JPG|Nagara Tomb No.16 長柄横穴群19号墓玄室.JPG| NagaraTomb No.19 </gallery>

The tombs have been opened since antiquity, and few {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been recovered, other than fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery and fragments of iron tools or weapons.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a 15-minute walk from the Tokumasu bus stop on the Kominato Bus from {{ill|Mobara Station|en|Mobara Station|WD=Q564427|lt=Mobara Station|1=Mobara Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Sotobo Line|Sotobo Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nagara Yokoanabo cluster | native_name =長柄横穴群 | native_name_lang = | image =長柄横穴群8号墓.JPG|image= File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagara Yokoanabo No.8 | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagara Yokoanabo cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|35|25|58|N|140|14|24|E||display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.432972|N|140.24025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|Nagara, Chiba|qid=Q1203545|lt=Nagara, Chiba|1=Nagara, Chiba}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Nagara Yokoanabo cluster'''|長柄横穴群|Nagara yokoana-gun}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|qid=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} containing a group of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs located in the Tokumasu neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|Nagara, Chiba|qid=Q1203545|lt=Nagara|1=Nagara, Chiba}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. These tombs have been protected as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1995.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162391|title=長柄横穴群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== Corridor-type ''kofun'' appeared in the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|qid=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō region|1=Bōsō Peninsula}} from the latter half of the 6th century AD and about 4000 examples have been found in Chiba Prefecture. The group at Nagara was made in the mid-7th and early 8th century AD, and consists of 324 tombs in 25 groups, distributed in the southern part of Nagara town. The style in this region is unique and is called the "Kodanshiki," in which the burial chamber is a smaller room located at the rear of an entrance room and elevated slightly above it. The largest tomb has difference in height between these rooms of 2.9 meters. The entrance rooms can roughly be divided into what is called an "arch type", in which the cross section of the ceiling shows a semi-cylindrical shape, and what is called a "house type" in which the ceiling and walls mimic the roof and walls of a house. In addition, there are a few which are "dome type" with a domed ceiling. Some of the tombs have a casket platform, on which the wooden sarcophagus once rested.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
One of the tombs, designated No.13, has a line engraving on its back wall depicting people, birds, houses, boats, and what appears to be a five-story [[Japanese pagoda]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tombs have been opened since antiquity, and few {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been recovered, other than fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery and fragments of iron tools or weapons.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a 15-minute walk from the Tokumasu bus stop on the Kominato Bus from {{ill|Mobara Station|en|Mobara Station|qid=Q564427|lt=Mobara Station|1=Mobara Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Sotobo Line|Sotobo Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
